body_txt: Shows set in the future will often have many things we have never imagined, but writers can't resist throwing in contemporary references, or at least references the viewers will understand. How do they accomplish this? Have one of the characters be a self-proclaimed expert of the past, e.g. 20th-to-21st-century Earth. A common variation (usually played for comedy) is to have the so-called "expert" be completely wrong about some major aspect of his subject-matter. This works particularly well when the subject is contemporary society, something that everybody living today knows about. If they're really wrong and mythologize said past, it veers into All Hail the Great God Mickey! Contrast with Disco Dan , except these people know they are not actually in the past. Compare with Born in the Wrong Century , Fantastic Anthropologist . See also The Genie Knows Jack Nicholson . Super-Trope of We Will All Be History Buffs in the Future (people in future settings know the past better than the average person today does).
